description_long: This is a simple, square wooden stool with a flat top surface and four straight legs. The legs are connected near the base by horizontal support bars, adding stability and a subtle design element. The stool has a uniform, matte beige or light brown finish, giving it a utilitarian and understated appearance. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments, emphasizing its functional design. The construction appears sturdy, suitable for supporting the weight of a seated person or objects placed on top. Its proportions and style suggest it would fit well in urban environments such as parks, bus stops, or public waiting areas.
description: A sturdy, square wooden stool with four legs and a matte beige finish, featuring simple horizontal support bars for stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat top, two front legs, and the horizontal support bars connecting the legs.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back legs and the arrangement of the support bars, with more of the under-structure visible.
description_view_2: Side view similar to the front, showing the flat top and the side profile of the support bars.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the flat top and the support bars, with a slightly different angle on the leg arrangement.
